title: Secret Bytes, Constant-Time Decisions, and Memory Lifecycle · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent team receives different assets, adversaries, data sizes, cryptoperiods, interoperability duties, availability limits, and compliance claims and transfers secret bytes, constant-time decisions, and memory lifecycle into a new secret custody and comparison protocol. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of secret custody and comparison protocol with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Trace secret creation, copies, aliases, use, retention, rotation, destruction limits, logs, crashes, backups, and ownership across Go boundaries.
Minimize conversions and copies of secret-bearing strings and byte slices while documenting unavoidable runtime limits.
Use constant-time comparison APIs for fixed-length secret-dependent values and reject length or parse errors without secret-dependent detail.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Crypto Go Secret Custody
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab cryptographic engineering team handles case 2144 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound secret copies, constant-time comparison scope, redaction, and honest erasure claims; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Crypto Go Secret Custody.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace secret creation, copies, aliases, use, retention, rotation, destruction limits, logs, crashes, backups, and ownership across go boundaries. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Crypto Go Secret Custody, keep Crypto Go Secret Custody connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

lab case — A lab cryptographic engineering team handles case 6096 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound secret copies, constant-time comparison scope, redaction, and honest erasure claims; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design useful logs, metrics, traces, dumps, and incident artifacts that never expose keys, plaintext, password material, tokens, or high-cardinality identities.
